rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT JOHN GEARY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. The distributions to the four beneficiaries of the Cantella/Cambridge Investment account were not exactly "in equal shares," as directed in the will, it is unclear why the executor had to wire funds for the credit union's claim instead of just writing a check, the "corrected" calculation of the statutory fee was incorrect (but not in favor of the attorney), yet another incorrect amount of the statutory fee was used in calculating the net distribution of the residue, and the amended prayer showed the wrong total amount of the attorney fees and costs. "Water" is not likely the name of any payee. (An order is "proposed" until it is signed by a judicial officer; there is no need to call it a "[Proposed] Order". All pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

The hearing was continued to 1/28/26 on 11/17/25. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DORIS JOHANNA MEHLER REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 17, 2024
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Property As Trust Assets (Probate Code 17200.1b, 17206 And 850 Ct. Seq.) (Estate Of Heggstad)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/17/26. (No earlier availability.) Probate Code Section 7051 pertains to proceedings concerning administration of the decedent's estate. There is no estate proceeding here. Probate Code Section 19003 pertains to a trustee's option to give notice to creditors. This is not a proposed notice to creditors; it is a petition filed by a trustee pursuant to Probate Code Section 850. Trust proceedings are governed by Probate Code Section 17000, et seq. Venue for trust proceedings is where the principal place of administration of the trust is located [Probate Code Section 17005(a)(1)]. The trustee resides in Millbrae. If the trustee is unable to obtain financial statements, how does she know that Doris Johanna Mehler owned them? The examiner has been unable to find a written consent from Nestor Bradley. It appears that the trustee's attorney signed his consent. If the petition is not dismissed to be filed in the proper venue, appearance will be required on 6/17/26. Courtesy copies of papers filed on or after 1/15/26 must be received by the assigned examiner by 6/3/26. Courtesy copies are FILE STAMPED, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Only one courtesy copy of the supplement to the petition, filed on 1/15/26, has been received by the examiner to date. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW J. GRIMSTAD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/20/26. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect creditors, even if the will waives, or all heirs waive, bond. Declaration verified by petitioner setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate to be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond must be filed [minumum bond is $20,000 - Local Rule 14.16F]. All documents to be filed by 5/4/26.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BERNICE DE LA JARA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/17/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Items 5a(3) or (4) and (7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (4) All persons mentioned in the will must be listed in item 8 of the petition, and all devisees must receive notice. [Probate Code Section 8110(b).] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/3/26. A verified supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper, should suffice to provide the information that was missing from the petition. Proof of service of notice to all devisees of the 6/17/26 hearing, with a printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice, must be filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
